prediction.reasoning: The prediction is based on the Geometric Brownian Motion model. Historical daily logarithmic returns for AMGN over the past year (approximately 252 trading days) were used to estimate daily drift (mean return) and daily volatility (standard deviation). The point estimate represents the median of the predicted log-normal price distribution after 6 trading days. Confidence intervals are calculated using the estimated drift, volatility, and appropriate Z-scores for each confidence level, assuming log-normal distribution of prices. This model assumes that past price movements are indicative of future movements and that volatility remains constant.
